title: Notational Intelligence, Linus Lee | Compile 26
summary: Linus Lee explores how the notations we use to write ideas shape our thinking and reasoning. He argues that deep learning models might discover entirely new notational systems that better capture abstract concepts and mathematical structures.
writeup: In this Compile 26 talk, Linus Lee examines notational intelligence: the idea that the notations used to write down ideas shape how thinking and reasoning proceed. The argument extends to deep learning models, which Lee suggests could discover entirely new notational systems better suited to capturing abstract concepts and mathematical structures. The framing connects intelligence, abstraction, and mathematical notation, treating the form of representation as a factor in reasoning rather than a neutral container.
